assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action\nSub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-1.md exists and references Comment ID 30001. report.md table row: '30001 | ... | code change request | Sub-task created'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: suggestion'. Reasoning section explicitly cites the suggestive language: '"should also add" (proposing an additional enhancement beyond the current scope) and "would help" (indicating a benefit rather than a requirement)'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and '## Action\nNo sub-task created.' No subtask file exists for comment 30003.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and '## Action\nNo sub-task created.' No subtask file exists for comment 30004.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 4), '## Files to Modify' (line 8), '## Implementation Notes' (line 11), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 17).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains '## Review Context' section (lines 27-32) with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File path, and the full original comment text quoted: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-1.md), and one report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) are present in the outputs. The report content shows analysis and classification activity only, with no indication of code modification.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md Review Comment Classifications table lists all four comments with their classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging. The overall result is 'FAIL' with issues requiring attention listed. The report concludes with an AI-generated comment attribution, not a merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file with 5 test functions covering delete, not-found, conflict, include_deleted listing, and cascade behavior. No existing test files were modified or deleted. |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility' section evaluating three checks: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check — no convention found, (2) Codebase pattern check — no consistent index creation pattern found, (3) Performance-related scrutiny — general best practices excluded as evidence. Conclusion: 'No convention upgrade is warranted.' report.md also contains a '### Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section for comment 30002 with the same findings.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Action\nNo sub-task created.' report.md table row: '30002 | ... | suggestion | No sub-task (no convention backs upgrade)'. Only one subtask file exists (subtask-1.md) and it is for comment 30001, not 30002.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews found on PR)'. The Test Quality overall is PASS, not affected by Eval Quality.
